mbox_from_line: From luci at powerneth.ro  Tue Aug 31 00:04:36 2021
subject: [patch] elfutils: enable host build
body_for_scoring:
frr 8.0 needs host libelf dev
add option for host build
tested on x86, ramips, kirkwood

Signed-off-by: Lucian Cristian <lucian.cristian at gmail.com>
-------------- next part --------------
[diff: package/libs/elfutils/Makefile]
[hunk: PKG_BUILD_DEPENDS:=!USE_GLIBC:argp-standalone]
+include $(INCLUDE_DIR)/host-build.mk
[hunk: else]
+HOST_CONFIGURE_ARGS += \
+	--disable-debuginfod \
+	--disable-libdebuginfod \
+	--without-lzma \
+	--without-zstd
[hunk: define Package/libelf/install]
+$(eval $(call HostBuild))
body_no_diff:
frr 8.0 needs host libelf dev

tested on x86, ramips, kirkwood

Signed-off-by: Lucian Cristian <lucian.cristian at gmail.com>

mbox_from_line: From luci at powerneth.ro  Fri Feb  5 10:32:38 2021
subject: OpenWrt add dependency to swig for U-Boot builds
body_for_scoring:
On 31.01.2021 20:43, Hauke Mehrtens wrote:
Hi,

Please do so, it's a small inconvenience oppose to always patch the 
u-boot sources

Regards,

Lucian
body_no_diff:
On 31.01.2021 20:43, Hauke Mehrtens wrote:
> The U-Boot build for more and more SoCs is using binman by default to 
> combine the images (SPL, U-Boot, ...). Binman is build from the U-Boot 
> project and it needs swig to build. We have multiple patches in OpenWrt 
> to remove this dependency from U-Boot, but it costs more and more time 
> to revert back to the old code. We have them in sunxi and rockchip 
> U-Boot, the Mediatek U-Boot build failed some time ago because of 
> missing swig in build bots.
> 
> I was just trying to update sunxi U-Boot to 2021.01 and the binman usage 
> changed again, so it needs more adaptations.
> 
> Building swig in OpenWrt tools will not be easy. We needs swig with 
> python bindings and this version needs the python development headers to 
> build.
> 
> I would like to add swig as an official dependency to OpenWrt, we could 
> make it depend on the target if this is possible.
> 
> Does anyone have an opinion on this topic?
> 
> Hauke
> 
> 
> _______________________________________________
> openwrt-devel mailing list
> openwrt-devel at lists.openwrt.org
> https://lists.openwrt.org/mailman/listinfo/openwrt-devel
> 
Hi,

Please do so, it's a small inconvenience oppose to always patch the 
u-boot sources

Regards,

Lucian

mbox_from_line: From arinc.unal at gmail.com  Sat Jul 24 10:55:42 2021
subject: [PATCH 21.02] mwlwifi: downgrade the 88W8964's firmware to 9.3.2.6 to prevent instability
body_no_diff:
> ?On Sat, Jul 24, 2021 at 6:40 PM Hauke Mehrtens <hauke at hauke-m.de> wrote:
>> 
>>> On 7/22/21 6:54 PM, Ar?n? ?NAL wrote:
>>> Avoid 88W8964 firmware 9.3.2.12 for Wi-Fi instabilities it causes on Linksys WRT3200ACM & WRT32X until fixes on master branch are backported to openwrt-21.02.
>> 
>> Which changes in master which are not backported are fixing this problem?
> 
> I don't know the specific commits, however, me and @rmandrad on GitHub
> under this PR (https://github.com/openwrt/openwrt/pull/4381) tried the
> master branch which includes the 9.3.2.12 firmware. Wireless works
> fine without any cut-out issues.
> We suspect it's the changes on the wireless stack (mac80211) that were
> not backported to openwrt-21.02 branch.
